title: Name/secure mandatory reviewers (SME per subject, a11y, bias, license)
context: Every question bank requires four mandatory, non-skippable human sign-offs before it can ship: a subject-matter reviewer (key + rationale correctness), a license reviewer (source permits derivatives), an accessibility reviewer (a11y conformance), and a bias/sensitivity reviewer. Additionally, a Steward must own partner relationships and record adoption evidence. Until these roles are named and confirmed for the pilot's subject area, the pilot bank (quiz-bank-open-pilot-009) cannot enter review and cannot ship. This is a blocking M0 prerequisite.
objective: Identify, recruit, and confirm the minimum set of mandatory reviewers required for the pilot bank and document their roles, contact information, and sign-off process.
acceptanceCriteria: At least one subject-matter reviewer (SME) confirmed for the pilot's subject area, with qualifications documented.
License reviewer, accessibility reviewer, and bias/sensitivity reviewer roles each filled by a named, confirmed individual.
Steward role identified (person responsible for partner relationships and recording adoption evidence).
Sign-off process documented: how reviewers are assigned to a bank, what they review, and how their approval is recorded as a committed artifact.
Document is committed to the project repo; CC-BY-4.0 licensed; commit is DCO signed-off.
resources: quiz-bank-open-a11ybias-005 (checklist — defines what accessibility and bias reviewers review)
quiz-bank-open-gate-003 (defines what the license reviewer checks)
output: A committed document naming all mandatory reviewers for the pilot bank, their roles, qualifications, and the sign-off process.
